THE FEBRUARY 22, 2021 TRIAL DATE IS VACATED. THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS FEBRUARY 8, 2021 AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(F) IS FEBRUARY 19, 2021. ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE

Appearances via Court Call: Thomas A. Stieg of Prindle Goetz Barnes & Reinholtz LLP (415.788.8354) for Consolidated Insulation, Inc.; Jonathan Kim of Foley & Mansfield PLLP (510.590.9500) for Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(e). Opposition filed by Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, LTD. Plaintiff moves for preference only under C.C.P. 36(e), not C.C.P. 36(d). C.C.P. 36(e) does not require Plaintiff to be 70 years of age or older or provide medical documentation showing substantial medical doubt of his survival beyond six months. Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, LTD failed to explain the relevance of the medical documents attached as Exhibit A to the declaration of Andrew L. Sharp.

The February 22, 2021 trial date is vacated. The new trial date is February 8, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 19, 2021. All pre-trial dates and deadlines remain dictated by the initial September 9, 2019 trial date.

Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 25, 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 8, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by January 29, 2021 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, February 4, 2021 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed. Judge: Cynthia M. Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/CML)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Continued To Oct-22-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference is continued on the Court's own motion from October 20, 2020 to October 22, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew L. Sharp, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD, Tel.510.590.9581, for defendant and moving party;
Kimberly J. Chu, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP, Tel.415.898.1555, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, as follows:

Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied on procedural grounds. Defendant fails to identify which causes of action are at issue in the notice and separate statement. (CRC rules 3.1350(b) and (d)(1).) Defendant further fails to follow the format for separate statements under CRC rule 3.1350(h). A "trial court has no burden to search through the parties' evidence for a triable issue of fact. The parties' separate statements are intended to permit the judge to determine quickly whether the motion is supported by sufficient undisputed facts." (St. Paul Mercury Ins. Co. v. Frontier Pac. Ins. Co. (2003) 111 Cal. App. 4th 1234, 1248, as modified on denial of reh'g (Aug. 28, 2003) (emphasis in original).) Additionally, Defendant has not established that its affirmative sophisticated intermediary defense would dispose of an entire cause of action. (C.C.P. 437c(f)(1).)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee; Clerk: G.Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sheila Pham CSR#13293, Tel.415.517.5439, email:spahm28@yahoo.com
